name: Spiced Lamb with Onions
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 kg Shoulder of lamb or mutton
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat into large cubes.
2. Slice half the onions finely and chop the rest.
3. Place the chopped onions in an electric blender with garlic, ginger, yoghurt, chilli powder, paprika, coriander leaves, ground coriander, and black cumin seeds. Blend until smooth.
4. Heat ghee and oil in a large heavy pan and fry the sliced onions, stirring frequently, until evenly browned. Remove from pan.
5. Add the cubed meat to the pan, not too many pieces at one time, and fry on high heat until browned on all sides. Remove each batch as it is browned and add more.
6. When all the meat has been cooked and removed from the pan, add a little more ghee or oil if necessary. Fry the blended mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it is cooked and gives off an aromatic smell. The oil should start to appear around the edges of the mixture.
7. Return the meat to the pan, add cardamom pods, and stir well. Cover and cook on low heat until the meat is almost tender. Stir occasionally. It may be necessary to add a little water, but usually, the juices from the meat are sufficient.
8. When the meat is tender and the liquid has been absorbed, add garam masala and the reserved fried onions. Replace the lid on the pan and leave on very low heat for an additional 15 minutes.
9. Serve with rice or Indian bread.
